rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

The hotel itself is nice. The room was clean and comfortable, the rooftop bar is awesome (although pricey for the quality- the sliders were delicious though), the food was overall good, and the valet service was great. The location though………. I don’t even know how to properly describe it in words. I had read the area is rough, with a lot of drug use and homelessness, but I was baffled. I know there is not really much the hotel can do about it, but I am kind of surprised that Marriott would establish a nice hotel in that area (which has been a dump for years). I am even more surprised this is part of The Edit Collection for Chase and The Hotel Collection for Amex. You simply cannot leave at night, and even during the day is questionable, especially if you’re not used to navigating major cities (aka- walking by homeless and drug users). Literally walked outside and about 10 feet away, there was a man without pants on, fully defecating in the middle of the sidewalk. It even looked exactly like the emoji. 💩 In a pile right outside the hotel. The entire surrounding area was exactly on par with Kensington in Philly IYKYK. Open air drug market, people actively using in broad daylight, needles on the ground, feces all over the place, and fenty-folds scattered all over the place like a zombie apocalypse. Overall inside the hotel is nice, but I would not recommend it to anyone I care about.

We are proud of the experience we create within our walls, and we remain committed to providing a safe, welcoming environment for every guest. I sincerely regret that the surrounding environment overshadowed what should have been a fully enjoyable stay.    If you would be open to it, I would welcome the opportunity to connect with you directly to discuss your experience further.    Properly,  Larisa Mereora  Director of Front Office  San Francisco Proper Hotel
Emergency cancelation - hotel management don’t care !

My husband booked a night for my 60th birthday along with my brother and his wife on separate bookings using Marriott points.  A day before we were to arrive, my sister in law had a medical emergency in the UK, her mother was taken ill and was in the hospital, so of corse all parties cancelled our booking and she flew to the UK. Apparently the hotel had a 72 hour cancellation in place, so charged us and my brother $220 as we only cancelled 24 hours before.  Both parties called the hotel to explain the situation and they could care less.  I understand policy, but under these circumstances, denying a cancellation is cruel.  My sister in law in particular had the cost of the last minute flight and then the hotel on top.  To say the least, we won’t be booking again.    There are many options of great hotels in San Francisco, and I would suggest if choosing a weekend away, you pick somewhere that respects its guests and are somewhat flexible should an emergency occur.  Not impressed.
Great hotel; neighborhood is a disaster

Hotel is great, neighborhood is a disaster area.   I am from an urban area and live downtown but this was way worse than I expected.  Website description of the neighborhood is misleading.   It is not an area for children, teenagers under 18, or anyone uncomfortable with significant urban squalor, e.g., very significant drug use and dealing, lots of mentally ill folks wandering around, streets dirty with everything from feces to needles, etc.   I didn't feel in danger per se, but it’s pretty nasty out there.  Women (or men, really) traveling alone may well feel uncomfortable or scared.   At minimum you’ll see some things you won’t be able to unsee.

Let’s start with valet parking — it took nearly 40 minutes just to figure out what was happening. The valet attendant repeatedly told me he couldn’t take my car because he was the only one working and that the lot was full. He even suggested I find my own parking several blocks away and walk back.    Then there’s the location. The area feels unsafe, especially at night — there are many homeless individuals around, and walking outside after dark is uncomfortable.    When I needed my car later, even though I scheduled pickup five hours in advance, it still wasn’t ready. The explanation? “It’s stuck in construction traffic.”    The room itself was fine but nothing special. My curtain jammed shut after closing it, so I couldn’t open it the next morning. Noise from the street — cars, people, dogs — made it hard to get a peaceful night’s sleep.    In short, I wouldn’t recommend this hotel. Between the parking chaos, poor location, and lack of comfort, it simply isn’t worth the time, money, or stress.

I was underwhelmed with my experience the moment I stepped out of my uber. The valet attendant didn't help with my bags down the three steps, he didn't even say hello he was just playing on his phone behind the counter. I checked in, went upstairs with my key and it didn't work on the door. I came back down, got my key switched, went upstairs and fobbed into my room... it was someone else's room. There was shoes and a bag in the room. I came back downstairs, the front desk didn't apologize, they didn't say what a weird thing that was to happen. They just swapped my key for a different room. This made me feel SO unsafe. Then my room itself, it didn't have a hairdryer and housekeeping brought me a broken one. There were no snacks in the mini bar, the phone in my room didn't work. I won't be staying here again - really disappointing for a 5 star hotel and honestly, a little dangerous.

I’m a frequent traveler but rarely take time to write a review. However based on the horrific experience at this hotel I felt compelled to. This hotel is in the WORST area of San Francisco. There are numerous homeless people outside, I I witnessed several people doing drugs openly on the street, there are needles on the ground, human feces, trash…need I go on?  I live in NYC so am accustomed to city living but this is on another level. I did not feel safe as a female traveler even in broad daylight and feel compelled to share with others. When I asked where I could go get coffee early one morning the front desk staff told me it was not safe to walk outside by myself. Unless you want to feel like a prisoner in your own hotel, book elsewhere. I absolutely WOULD NOT RECOMMEND.   As a Titanium Elite Member, Marriott needs to reconsider this location, I would expect more from them.

Good: Really smelled bad, awful facilities, and overpriced for what you get (bed bugs)    Bad: The smell, it’s awful. I’m not sure what is happening around or within the property but it smelled like a dead animal in my room. Asked to change rooms and they wouldn’t accommodate. Wild experience.

The Clift Royal Sonesta Hotel  San Francisco, CA

The hotel itself is nice. The room was clean and comfortable, the rooftop bar is awesome (although pricey for the quality- the sliders were delicious though), the food was overall good, and the valet service was great. The location though………. I don’t even know how to properly describe it in words. I had read the area is rough, with a lot of drug use and homelessness, but I was baffled. I know there is not really much the hotel can do about it, but I am kind of surprised that Marriott would establish a nice hotel in that area (which has been a dump for years). I am even more surprised this is part of The Edit Collection for Chase and The Hotel Collection for Amex. You simply cannot leave at night, and even during the day is questionable, especially if you’re not used to navigating major cities (aka- walking by homeless and drug users). Literally walked outside and about 10 feet away, there was a man without pants on, fully defecating in the middle of the sidewalk. It even looked exactly like the emoji. 💩 In a pile right outside the hotel. The entire surrounding area was exactly on par with Kensington in Philly IYKYK. Open air drug market, people actively using in broad daylight, needles on the ground, feces all over the place, and fenty-folds scattered all over the place like a zombie apocalypse. Overall inside the hotel is nice, but I would not recommend it to anyone I care about.
Very friendly and helpful staff, front desk, house keeping and the valet!  It’s a very cozy hotel with very modern interiors in a central location steps away from cafes, restaurants etc
Charmaine's rooftop bar had a lovely event, great food,views servers, great sales director.

Excellent hotel. Inside is very nice with a mixture of modern and historical elements. Loved the rooms, very spacious with great views. The room windows inside and outside were slightly dirty (trying to not nitpick) and would be nice if they were cleaned on the regular to improve views. The hotel needs more bellman carts, as for when I arrived (when it was not crowded) they didn't have one available to me. The food at roof top restuarant was good, but the menu is limited and the portion sizes are small. The neighborhood around the hotel is not the cleanest or safest, so plan to take an Uber or Lyft instead of walking by foot. Plan to valet your car, and beware of about $100 overnight charge for that. Overall, great stay and great hotel. Pleasantly surprised and will be back.
Everything and everyone was amazing. The food and drinks and hospitality on the rooftop restaurant/bar were exceptional. I would stay at the hotel again.
A real gem of a hotel.  Decor was exquisite throughout.  Extremely friendly staff all the way through.  Beautiful rooftop bar.
Located just down the street from the Orpheum Theater. Great hotel, the roof top bar is amazing. Staff is terrific, they really go out of their way to serve you.

This was the poorest stay I've had in a while. I was not recognized as a Titanium Elite at check-in. I was told I had late check in through my AmEx reservation, but then I was told I did not. There were no face cloths in my room. My sink backed up. Hot water was spotty. There was only one trash can and no recycle bin in the room, and the lighting was awful. The restaurant hours appeared to be completely random. Honestly, there is nothing about the state that I enjoyed and for a "Proper" hotel, the experience was incredibly disappointing.

Room was well designed, complete with Aesop amenities and comfortable bedding. Staff, food and onsite gym were great.  Only knocked a point due to the unhoused population that surround the hotel, which at times feels unsafe. Something to consider if you’re traveling alone / nature of your trip to SF.
Fantastic hotel. Great food, amazing rooms and centrally located.

While location isn’t the nicest area of Market St, it is a great location to get around the city. Charlemagne’s is a great roof top bar. Staff is excellent. Villon breakfast was delicious. Valet is on McAlister as you turn right off of 7th. If you get it wrong it’s a long loop around in traffic.

The level of service and personalized attention made our stay a first rate experience!  My first stay at The SF Proper has set the bar high - from reservation to reception to dining….   Clearly they hire exceptionally skilled and talented staff who work with attentive professionalism  to provide their guests with a fabulous experience at this beautiful boutique hotel.

The S.F. hotel lobby is gorgeous but the neighborhood is ugly and dangerous. Parking is quite expensive and the valet is not as professional as expected. The rooms are small and cramped (not really cozy). Rooftop bar was closed due to buy out which we were not informed of ahead of time before check in.

Fantastic boutique decor and exceptional service.  Rooftop bar and restaurants were beautiful and very accessible as a hotel guest.  Agreed that the block it’s on is unfortunately very shady.  There was very helpful security guarding the front door and I only walked in daylight (and only on to Market) and always just ubered back. I’m glad I have this location a chance and it was a great stay!

This hotel is nice, service and staff 10/10. The breakfast was decent but seemed overpriced. Room service dessert was a bit underwhelming and the lobby coffee was awful. I loved the interior of the hotel and the gym is super nice. Unfortunately the location is the reason I wouldn’t stay here again.

Possibly the best hotel I’ve ever been to. Especially because the rooftop bar Charmaine has amazing food and drinks! Wemerson, a bartender at the roof, especially provided both 10/10 customer service and drinks. Strongly recommend.

Great hotel. Helpful and friendly staff, lovely rooms, extra touches and rooftop bar were excellent. Vegan breakfast option available (overnight oats) as well as vegan snacks on the rooftop bar (guac and chips), olives, nuts). Super close to music venues.

Despite the surround area, the hotel itself is very welcoming and everyone very friendly and above and beyond to make you feel welcome. Rooms were clean and good amenities. I didn’t have a reservation for the rooftop but the team squeezed me in - great views and cocktails!

They downgraded our room when checking in without telling us. I was surprised by the room and sure enough it was a cheaper room than we had paid in full for. They offered me credit to spend at the restaurant for $100. Then they didn’t have room to seat us to use the credit. Finally it was resolved but upon checkout there was no credit applied. So I again had to deal with the gm to get it resolved. Things happen but staff was not helpful or apologetic. This hotel was not worth the money.

I went to get some drinks with my fiancé and left very satisfied due to bartender Jaime! He’s such a gentleman and made sure we were very much satisfied. That man can work👏!  I hope to keep coming and receiving the same service from Jaime. Thank you Jaime and Proper hotel for the amazing experience!😊

I thought you said this would be easy. Too many comments.  The surrounding occupants made the SF experience less.  I needed to take a Lyft to the Embarcadero.  I'm not sure why I was not allowed to drive up Market Street.

Staff barely seemed interested in guest experience. Room phone did not work, zero assistance with bags on the stairs in, no explanation of any features or services available, room service was lacking, response from front desk was lackadaisical. GM sort of followed up, but never responded to questions. Cute property, but no substance. Would not stay again.

Stayed here for the second time due to proximity to the Orpheum Theater in SF and the SF city hall and park.  Lots of very colorful people around.  We love this hotel!  Front staff is awesome, ya get a flute of champagne as you check in, then great service - room wasn't ready (we go there early); they gave us recommendations for lunch place.  2 hours later got a text our room was ready; when we came back our bags were in the room - great touch.  Rooms are great.  Location is awesome though, and easy to grab an Uber or Waymo to jet around the city.  Found an awesome restaurant called Sam's just beyond the Orpheum (5 minute walk, on Market) and was again surprised as to the quality of this breakfast lunch place - it does get a bit busy just before show time.  Did not get a chance to have a cocktail at the top of the Proper; we did that last time and it was enjoyed!

Worst possible location in San Francisco. In the middle of Tenderloin. To avoid at all cost!!  The interiors of the hotel are actually nice although the rooms are extremely extremely small. Not what you would expect from a Marriott property. The rooftop bar is 5 stars!  The personnel is also very nice and willing to help. I would not go back not even for free.

I stayed at Proper Hotwl last night and couldn’t be happier. The hotel is very stylish, artistic, and spotless. I really liked the Aesop brand amenities, the shampoo, creams, and hand wash were a nice touch. The rooftop bar Charmaine’s is one of my favorite spots in San Francisco, with great vibes and charm. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and overall, it was a fun and pleasant stay.

I love this hotel. I stay for work 2x a year and never have had a single complaint or hiccup. The staff makes sure every aspect of your stay is comfortable and feels like home. Theresa at Villion starts my day with a huge smile + warmth in her service. Every evening, Jose at Charmaine’s was recommending me the best drink + food combinations and glorifying the incredible chefs newest creations. On top of the incredible service, the hotel is decorated in superb fashion, every room is a bit different, making it feel more boutique than corporate. I travel to Palo Alto, Larkspur, Walnut Creek, and this location is great for renting a car off Mason street + more central to all my other towns I have to visit. Can’t wait to come back next season.

I was underwhelmed with my experience the moment I stepped out of my uber. The valet attendant didn't help with my bags down the three steps, he didn't even say hello he was just playing on his phone behind the counter. I checked in, went upstairs with my key and it didn't work on the door. I came back down, got my key switched, went upstairs and fobbed into my room... it was someone else's room. There was shoes and a bag in the room. I came back downstairs, the front desk didn't apologize, they didn't say what a weird thing that was to happen. They just swapped my key for a different room. This made me feel SO unsafe. Then my room itself, it didn't have a hairdryer and housekeeping brought me a broken one. There were no snacks in the mini bar, the phone in my room didn't work. I won't be staying here again - really disappointing for a 5 star hotel and honestly, a little dangerous.

2 friends and I stayed at the Proper over the weekend in order to attend the Wyld Hunt Fae Ball happening across the street at the Hibernia. We were able to check in early, the room was just the right size for all 3 of us, and the hotel itself is walking distance from so many lovely little cafes and restaurants, as well as BART.    The valet parking was also quite nice, so that we didn't have to worry about trying to find parking nearby.    We would definitely stay here again!

Beautifully decorated hotel with comfortable room and excellent restaurants. Our room was on the smaller side, but very functional with nice amenities and decor. Breakfast in the hotel restaurant was some of the best I’ve ever had at a hotel. We enjoyed the rooftop deck with our own fire pit, great cocktails and lovely view.  Clientele skewed mostly 20’s to 50’s and the vibe was upscale. The location of the hotel, while convenient, gave us pause, but there were no issues — we walked during the day and took Ubers at night. Would stay here again.
Best service! Gracias a Sergio the bartender 🙌🏻🙏🏻

We had dinner the first night and there were only five things on the menu and we had to eat in the lobby because Charmaine‘s and the Villon restaurant were both handling private groups.  I honestly have never stated a hotel and had to eat in the lobby.  And the front desk staff to be helpful made us a reservation at Charmaine’s for our second night, but then we found out right before that the rooftop was actually closed and our reservations were invalid. Altogether rather frustrating.
always wonderful. The staff is impeccable.
wonderful service from the young man who’s name starts with an N. Both times i visited he was so accommodating and lovely to chat with.
Menu was a little limited but we liked what we had
The food was terrible and way overpriced for what it was. And we are not some Out of Towners that are shocked by San Francisco prices. We are Foodies, the food quality was very poor.
As mentioned above, new hire Asian gentleman had amazing service
It would’ve been nice to have fresh squeezed, orange juice, and instead of just the bottle stuff from behind the bar besides that everything was delicious. The polenta meal was outstanding.
It would be nice if there were some sort of room service food options between 2p and 5p

We had breakfast there. The food was good, but there weren't enough servers on duty, and it took a long time for the server to provide us with our check, after flagging them down. Every table in our section had a similar experience.
Thought very friendly staff, service was slow--multiple tables were waiting for any person to bring check to table.

I had not initially realized that my room was designated for individuals with disabilities. Unfortunately, the shower did not have a water guard at the bottom, which resulted in some water leakage into the bathroom; I understand this may be a requirement for accessibility. Additionally, it would be helpful to have a dresser in the room for organizing my clothing, rather than having to stack it in the closet. Thank you for considering these suggestions.
We had room service. Food was delicious apart from the large egg shell in the shakshuka that was mentioned above. Prices are also steep.
It was a bit pricey. My husband had the waffle which was overly sweet and difficult to eat. I only had a smoothie which ended up costing around $20.
Probably needs one more host staff, but everyone was kindl and helpful.

Are the apples and orange juice infused? Every time I got them there was a weird taste. Also the waffles didn’t clarify they were stroompf-waffle-esque and not fluffy. Also the high chair is missing a piece that holds smaller babies (not toddlers) in place
Jim did a great job of taking care of us

I eat at both restaurants every time I stay, my only ask is for better wine options. Also, the mini bar in the room truffle chips are the BEST, and there used to me more local snacks included - like beef jerky, and also a fun kink box. Those sorts of add on's I enjoyed! Bring 'em back.
I wish they had a better mocktail selection.
Staff were friendly but often distracted talking to each other with their backs to customers. Hard to get their attention.
Jim was great, exceptional service! Thank you!
The menu was a little eclectic. But I was able to find something I liked.
Menu options could be better. Limited options.
It smelled bad by the time we got there at 11:30
Food was good, quick and service was as expected.
We were really happy it was open until midnight. When we came back from our evening out it was  a lovely way to end the night
I mentioned this above, but it seems like there wasn't a fourth Sunday rooftop layover Happy hour as mentioned on your website, which was a small disappointment.
Trout dip was oily and lacked in flavour. Drinks were good.
Norma and her team were amazing. They catered our wedding and were very attentive to our needs and made everyone feel comfortable.
AMAZING! The staff works so had to make it an exceptional experience.
The food wasn't very warm, if at all. Also, for us, the kitchen opens too late, 7am.  We are early risers.
It would be nice if there were some sort of room service food options between 2p and 5p .
The food ment to be hot was cold

when asking for extra ketchup would it be too difficult to just include the bottle? I asked for extra ketchup the first time and got two little metal containers. The second time I asked for extra, extra ketchup and got the same amount.

The room service staff walked into the room to collect payment even though I told them to not enter since my husband was showering.     There was a big hair in our food but it was promptly replaced when reported.
Ordered the fish & chips which were overly salty to the point where you couldn’t eat them

It was unclear how long the food would take to arrive. Given our experience with the toothbrush request, we had lost trust in the hotel's ability to answer requests and we wanted more information to ensure the food was being prepared and sent to our room.
There was no all day dining, which was surprising considering the number of restaurants and status of the hotel. There was no food available between 2pm and 5pm.
Great room service! Same menu/kitchen as main restaurant, so we knew what worked. Good menu - We needed gluten free, you were able to accommodate well.
ordered dessert.  Tiramisu was great.  Red Velvet whoopie pies were frozen solid.  Once thawed, cake was dry.
Through Villon - the salad with chicken was very impressive, as were the olives!
weren't serving several items and very expensive for what we received

I had Charmaine's for dinner via room service, and was disappointed by the limited range of vegetarian options. The sweet potato quesadilla was reasonably good, but would not have been my first choice given other viable options.
